mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price indices indicate strong demand outpacing supply constraints, which forces corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and CPIAUCSL yields negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production directly increases short-term corporate output and earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day SPX returns when FEDFUNDS is rising, or the predictive IC drops below 0.01 across rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects robust aggregate economic activity that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no correlation with 60-day SPX returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 310.0, it triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 310.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and corporate revenue, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term lacks predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer balance sheets and reduced construction activity, which drags on economic growth and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days during credit-constrained environments.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to predict negative 60-day SPX returns when credit spreads are narrow, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 45%.
